script:
Back when I was in college, my laptop had this annoying issue where the keyboard randomly typed extra characters.
Most of the time it was just gibberish, so I assumed the keyboard was wearing out.
One night I was finishing an essay at around 2 AM.
Every few minutes my screen would fill with random letters like “asdlfkajsd.”
I kept deleting them and working.
Then it typed something different.
Not gibberish.
A full sentence: “ARE YOU ALONE?”
I stared at the screen, confused.
I backspaced it and kept typing.
A minute later, new text appeared: “WHY ARE YOU IGNORING ME?”
I closed all my programs and restarted the laptop.
As soon as it booted, more text appeared on a blank screen: “DON’T MAKE ME ASK AGAIN.”
I slammed the lid shut.
After a while I opened it again.
Nothing typed this time, but all the lights in my dorm room were off even though I never turned them off.
I shut the laptop down and left it unplugged for two days.
When I finally turned it on again, everything worked normally.
No typing.
No weird messages.
But in my Documents folder, there was a new file.
Just one line inside it:
“THANK YOU FOR LISTENING.”
